frameworks.name: phasic arousal / brainstem neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: phasic brainstem arousal responses suppress trial-by-trial decision bias

frameworks.name: signal-detection / decision bias
frameworks.one_line: choice variability decomposed into bias modulated by arousal state

frameworks.name: noise in decision-making machinery
frameworks.one_line: alternative account attributing choice variability to internal noise

core_question: Do phasic responses of brainstem arousal systems account for trial-by-trial variability in perceptual choice bias?
key_assumptions: pupil response is a valid proxy of phasic arousal
decision bias can be isolated from sensory evidence on a trial-by-trial basis
brainstem neuromodulatory centers (e.g., locus coeruleus) drive cortical arousal state
manipulations: trials sorted by magnitude of pupil response
key_findings: large pupil responses predict reduced decision bias
pupil-linked bias reduction co-varies with choice-encoding patterns in parietal and prefrontal cortex
phasic brainstem responses (including noradrenergic locus coeruleus) predict the pupil-linked bias reduction
interpretations: phasic arousal suppresses decision bias on a trial-by-trial basis
brainstem arousal systems are a significant source of choice variability beyond pure decision noise
